summary: Verified two content edits on /app/frontend/index.html: (1) CR1 & CR3 dimension format standardized to inches-first (cm in parens), CR2 preserved; (2) homepage FAQ answer for 'What is rotomold construction?' replaced with new roto-mold copy. Ran 53 new pytest cases covering master HTML, all 6 pre-rendered outputs, FAQPage JSON-LD, regression on _redirects (20 rules) / _headers (15 blocks) / offers block, and live preview URL. Also re-ran prior 3 test suites (71 tests). All 124 tests pass.
critical_code_review_comments: index.html is a single 3000+ line monolith mixing HTML, CSS, JS routing and JSON-LD. Long-term maintainability would benefit from splitting per-page templates and shared metadata, but this is a static marketing site so risk is low.

Some FAQ answers duplicate spec-table data (e.g. CR3 dims appear both in spec table and in an FAQ answer at line 2513). Not a bug but a small DRY concern — a source-of-truth object consumed by both blocks could avoid future divergence.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Content-verification test suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_content_updates.py. It covers dimension strings, FAQ copy, FAQPage JSON-LD, _redirects/_headers counts, offers block regression, and preview URL live checks. If master index.html copy changes again, update the string constants at the top of that file and re-run. Preview URL: https://cba431d2-474c-4b3c-8ad3-a929fb18ea97.preview.emergentagent.com.
rca of the issue: N/A — content-edit verification only. All expected strings present in master and pre-rendered outputs; all old strings removed; preview serves updated content; JSON-LD FAQPage picked up new answer via build-static.js extractFaqs().
